title: Purged group CV plus regime-specific sigmoid-calibrated ensemble
summary: This task has strong within-race dependence, so optimistic local CV can come from race-group leakage even when features are legal.
rationale: This task has strong within-race dependence, so optimistic local CV can come from race-group leakage even when features are legal. For deployment, sklearn notes sigmoid calibration preserves ranking, while isotonic can change AUC via ties. That makes a purged grouped OOF pipeline plus regime-wise sigmoid calibration attractive for ROC-AUC submissions.
implementation_hint: Model selection should use LeaveOneGroupOut or GroupKFold on Year_Race, optionally with purging of adjacent same-driver laps around validation races if any history features are used. Build 2-3 heterogeneous models, generate strict OOF predictions, then fit separate Platt/sigmoid calibrators for regimes such as dry vs wet and early/mid/late race. Blend calibrated OOF outputs only after all base predictions are fully OOF.
expected_effect: Likely improves LB alignment more than raw CV, and can turn a flat ensemble into a slightly stronger, stabler submission without changing base feature logic much.
risk: More pipeline complexity; if regime buckets are too small, calibration noise can outweigh the benefit.
